steps.title: Use the public's ranking
steps.content: The conclusion is about what the general public regards as important. The supporting evidence must therefore show how the general public ranked improvement of attitudes and outlooks, not how project leaders or stakeholders ranked it.

steps.title: Reject off-target comparisons
steps.content: Choice A discusses physical health instead of attitudes and outlooks. Choice B reverses the ranking because raw materials ranked lower for the general public. Choice C compares stakeholders with the public, but it does not prove the public's own prioritization. Choice D shows the general public ranked improvement of attitudes and outlooks above the other four listed services. The answer is D.
